enrichment.short_description: Adobe Acrobat is a PDF platform for viewing, editing, converting, e-signing, and organizing PDF files, available as a free Reader and paid Standard/Pro subscriptions.
enrichment.seo_title: Adobe Acrobat: PDF Editor, Converter & E-Sign
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Adobe Acrobat lets you view, edit, convert, and e-sign PDF files. Free Reader plus Standard and Pro plans starting at $14.99/month.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adobe Acrobat is a PDF document platform made by Adobe Inc., the San Jose, California software company founded in 1982. Acrobat lets users view, create, edit, convert, organize, protect, and electronically sign PDF files. The free Acrobat Reader app is used to open and annotate PDFs, while paid Acrobat Standard and Pro subscriptions unlock editing tools such as converting PDFs to and from Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, combining and reordering pages, redacting sensitive content (Pro), and applying passwords or permissions. Acrobat also includes an AI Assistant that can summarize documents and answer questions about PDF content, and integrates with Adobe Acrobat Sign for requesting and tracking electronic signatures. Acrobat is available as a desktop app for Windows and Mac, a web app, and mobile apps for iOS and Android, with documents synced through Adobe Document Cloud. Pricing is subscription-based: Acrobat Standard starts at $14.99/month and Acrobat Pro at $19.99/month on annual plans billed monthly, with higher month-to-month rates and separate team pricing. Acrobat is used by individuals, students, and businesses that need reliable PDF creation, conversion, and e-signature workflows, and it remains one of the most widely used PDF tools because the PDF format itself originated at Adobe. It competes with PDF tools such as Foxit, Nitro, and Smallpdf, but is often preferred for format fidelity and Creative Cloud integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adobe Acrobat is the PDF platform built by Adobe, the company that created the PDF format itself. It covers the full lifecycle of a PDF document, from viewing and annotating to editing, converting, organizing, and signing.
The free Acrobat Reader app handles viewing, printing, and basic commenting, while paid Acrobat Standard and Pro subscriptions add editing, conversion, and advanced document-protection tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Acrobat lets users edit text and images directly in a PDF, convert PDFs to and from Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, and merge, split, reorder, or compress files.

An AI Assistant can summarize long documents and answer questions about their content, and Acrobat Sign integration lets users request, track, and collect electronic signatures without leaving the app.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: Acrobat Standard starts at $14.99/month and Acrobat Pro at $19.99/month on annual plans billed monthly; month-to-month pricing without an annual commitment is higher. Team and business pricing is available separately.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Acrobat is used by businesses that route contracts and reports as PDFs, legal and sales teams that need e-signatures, and students or researchers converting scanned or PDF material into editable Office documents.

features.title: PDF Editing
features.description: Edit text and images directly within PDF files.

features.title: Convert PDFs
features.description: Convert PDFs to and from Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and image formats.

features.title: E-signatures
features.description: Request, sign, and track signatures via Acrobat Sign integration.

features.title: AI Assistant
features.description: Summarize documents and ask questions about PDF content using generative AI.

features.title: Organize & Protect
features.description: Merge, split, reorder, compress, and password-protect PDFs.

features.title: Free Reader
features.description: Acrobat Reader is free for viewing, printing, and basic annotation.

features.title: Cross-Platform Access
features.description: Desktop apps for Windows and Mac, plus web, iOS, and Android apps.

pros: Free Reader is ubiquitous and compatible with virtually all PDF files
Built-in e-signature workflow via Acrobat Sign
Reliable PDF-to-Office conversion accuracy
Available across desktop, web, and mobile
cons: Full editing and Pro-tier features require a paid subscription
Annual plans carry an early-cancellation fee
Can feel feature-heavy compared to lightweight PDF tools for simple tasks

faqs.question: Is Adobe Acrobat free?
faqs.answer: Acrobat Reader is free for viewing and basic annotation; editing, converting, and Pro features require a Standard or Pro subscription.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Acrobat Standard and Pro?
faqs.answer: Pro adds features like redaction, PDF comparison, and additional export/OCR options beyond what Standard offers.

faqs.question: Does Acrobat support e-signatures?
faqs.answer: Yes, through its integration with Adobe Acrobat Sign.

faqs.question: Can I cancel my Acrobat subscription anytime?
faqs.answer: Annual plans billed monthly charge an early-termination fee if canceled before the year is up; month-to-month plans can be canceled anytime at a higher price.

use_cases.title: Business document management
use_cases.description: Teams use Acrobat to edit, convert, and route contracts and reports as PDFs.

use_cases.title: Legal and contract e-signing
use_cases.description: Attorneys and sales teams use Acrobat Sign integration to collect signatures.

use_cases.title: Converting scanned or PDF documents
use_cases.description: Converting scanned or PDF materials into editable Word or Excel files.

enrichment.short_description: Adobe Express is a free online design app for creating social posts, flyers, videos, and marketing graphics using templates and built-in generative AI tools.
enrichment.seo_title: Adobe Express: Free Online Design & Video Tool
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Create social posts, flyers, and videos with Adobe Express. Free plan available; Premium adds AI credits and stock assets from $9.99/month.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adobe Express is a web and mobile design application made by Adobe Inc., built for quickly creating social media graphics, flyers, presentations, videos, and marketing materials from templates. It combines a drag-and-drop editor with generative AI features powered by Adobe Firefly, including text-to-image generation, background removal, and AI-assisted layout suggestions. The free plan includes access to over 100,000 templates, more than 1 million stock assets, 5GB of storage, and scheduling to one social account per network. The Premium plan, priced at $9.99 per month, expands this to over 200 million stock assets, 30,000+ fonts, roughly 250 monthly generative AI credits, video background removal, brand kits, 100GB of storage, and multi-account social scheduling; Premium is also bundled into Creative Cloud Standard and Pro subscriptions. A Teams plan starting around $4.99 per user per month adds collaboration and admin controls. Adobe Express runs in the browser and has dedicated iOS and Android apps. It's aimed at small businesses, marketers, content creators, students, and social media managers who need fast, template-driven design without the learning curve of full desktop tools like Photoshop or Illustrator.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adobe Express is a browser and mobile design app aimed at quick, template-driven content creation rather than the precision editing of Photoshop or Illustrator.
It blends a drag-and-drop editor, a large template and stock library, and generative AI tools powered by Adobe Firefly.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Users start from templates for social posts, flyers, presentations, and videos, and can generate images or remove backgrounds using built-in Firefly-powered AI tools.
A video editor supports trimming, captions, and background removal, and brand kits keep colors, fonts, and logos consistent across designs.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: The free plan includes 100,000+ templates, 1M+ stock assets, and 5GB of storage. Premium costs $9.99/month and adds expanded stock, fonts, generative AI credits, brand kits, and 100GB storage. A Teams plan starts around $4.99 per user/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Adobe Express is aimed at social media managers, small business owners, content creators, and students who need fast, on-brand graphics and video without a steep learning curve.

features.title: Template Library
features.description: 100,000+ templates for social posts, flyers, video, and presentations.

features.title: Generative AI Tools
features.description: Text-to-image generation and AI-assisted design powered by Adobe Firefly.

features.title: Stock Assets
features.description: Access to Adobe Stock photos, videos, icons, and fonts.

features.title: Social Scheduling
features.description: Schedule and publish posts directly to social accounts.

features.title: Brand Kits
features.description: Save brand colors, fonts, and logos for consistent designs (Premium).

features.title: Video Editor
features.description: Trim, add captions, and remove backgrounds from video clips.

features.title: Cross-Platform
features.description: Web app plus iOS and Android mobile apps.

pros: Free tier is genuinely usable with a large template and stock library
Generative AI features are built in without needing separate tools
Faster, more beginner-friendly editor than full Creative Cloud apps
Premium is included free with Creative Cloud Standard/Pro subscriptions
cons: Advanced design control is limited compared to Photoshop or Illustrator
Best AI and stock features are gated behind the Premium plan
Free plan limits storage and social scheduling to one account per network

faqs.question: Is Adobe Express free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Adobe Express has a free plan with templates, basic editing, and limited stock assets and storage.

faqs.question: What does Adobe Express Premium include?
faqs.answer: Premium ($9.99/month) adds expanded stock assets, more fonts, generative AI credits, brand kits, and more storage.

faqs.question: Is Adobe Express the same as Photoshop?
faqs.answer: No, Express is a template-based design tool for quick graphics and video, while Photoshop is a professional pixel-editing application.

faqs.question: Does Adobe Express have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's available on iOS and Android in addition to the web app.

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Marketers and creators use templates to produce on-brand social graphics and short videos quickly.
use_cases.audience: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Small business marketing materials
use_cases.description: Small businesses design flyers, logos, and promotional graphics without hiring a designer.

use_cases.title: Quick presentations and documents
use_cases.description: Users create presentations, resumes, and other documents from templates.

enrichment.short_description: Adobe Firefly is Adobe's generative AI tool for creating images, text effects, vector graphics, and video from text prompts, with a free tier and credit-based paid plans.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (limited monthly credits); paid plans from ~$9.99/month
enrichment.seo_title: Adobe Firefly: Generative AI Image & Video Tool
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Adobe Firefly generates images, video, and vector art from text prompts. Free plan available; paid plans start around $9.99/month.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adobe Firefly is a family of generative AI models and a web/mobile application built by Adobe Inc. that creates images, text effects, vector graphics, and video from text prompts. It powers generative features embedded across Creative Cloud apps such as Photoshop's Generative Fill and Illustrator's Generative Recolor, and is also available as a standalone web app and mobile app for iOS and Android. Firefly supports text-to-image generation, generative fill and expand, generative video (including via third-party models like Google's Veo), sound-effect generation, and Firefly Boards, a collaborative AI moodboarding tool in beta. Adobe states that Firefly's core models are trained on Adobe Stock images, openly licensed content, and public-domain material, and are designed to be safe for commercial use. Firefly uses a credit-based pricing system: a free plan includes a limited number of monthly generative credits, while paid plans (Standard, Pro, and Premium) offer larger monthly credit allotments for premium features like video and third-party AI models, with unlimited standard image generation included on paid tiers. Firefly credits are also bundled into Creative Cloud subscriptions at lower allocations. It's aimed at designers, marketers, content creators, and Creative Cloud users who want AI-assisted image and video generation integrated into their existing workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adobe Firefly is Adobe's generative AI platform, available as a standalone web and mobile app and embedded inside Creative Cloud apps like Photoshop and Illustrator.
Adobe states Firefly's models are trained on Adobe Stock, licensed, and public-domain content, positioning them as commercially safe to use.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Firefly generates images, text effects, and vector graphics from text prompts, and its Generative Fill/Expand tools extend or edit existing images inside Photoshop.
Firefly also generates short video clips and sound effects from text or image prompts, and Firefly Boards offers AI-assisted moodboarding for creative teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: A free plan offers a limited number of monthly generative credits. Paid plans (Standard, Pro, Premium) start around $9.99/month and scale up to larger monthly premium-credit allotments for video and partner-model generations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Firefly is aimed at designers, marketers, and content creators who want AI image/video generation, as well as existing Creative Cloud users who use Firefly-powered tools inside Photoshop and Illustrator.

features.title: Text-to-Image Generation
features.description: Generate images from text prompts.

features.title: Generative Fill & Expand
features.description: Extend or edit images with AI, integrated directly into Photoshop.

features.title: Generative Video
features.description: Create short video clips from text or image prompts.

features.title: Text Effects & Vector Generation
features.description: Stylize text and generate vector graphics from prompts.

features.title: Sound Effect Generation
features.description: Generate sound effects from text descriptions and add them to a timeline.

features.title: Firefly Boards
features.description: AI-assisted moodboarding for creative teams (public beta).

features.title: Mobile App
features.description: Standalone iOS and Android apps for on-the-go generation.

pros: Adobe states models are trained with commercial-safe use in mind
Deep integration with Photoshop, Illustrator, and Express
Free tier available to try core features
Supports multiple generation types beyond images, including video and audio
cons: Credit system can be confusing and premium features consume credits quickly
Free plan has a low monthly credit allowance
Output quality still depends on prompting skill, as with most generative AI tools

faqs.question: Is Adobe Firefly free?
faqs.answer: Yes, there's a free plan with a limited number of monthly generative credits.

faqs.question: What can Adobe Firefly generate?
faqs.answer: Images, text effects, vector graphics, video clips, and sound effects from text prompts.

faqs.question: Is Adobe Firefly integrated into Photoshop?
faqs.answer: Yes, Firefly powers features like Generative Fill directly inside Photoshop and other Creative Cloud apps.

faqs.question: Does Firefly have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Adobe launched standalone Firefly apps for iOS and Android in 2025.

use_cases.title: Rapid concept and image generation
use_cases.description: Designers generate visual concepts from text prompts before refining them in other tools.

use_cases.title: AI video creation
use_cases.description: Creators generate short video clips from text or images for social content.
use_cases.audience: Content creators and social media teams

use_cases.title: In-app photo editing
use_cases.description: Photoshop and Illustrator users use Firefly-powered Generative Fill and Generative Recolor to speed up edits.

enrichment.short_description: Adobe Illustrator is professional vector graphics software for creating logos, icons, illustrations, and typography, available as a Creative Cloud subscription.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Adobe Illustrator is the industry-standard vector design app for logos, icons, and illustration. Plans start around $22.99/month.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adobe Illustrator is a professional vector graphics editor made by Adobe Inc., first released in 1987, used for designing logos, icons, typography, illustrations, and print or digital artwork built from scalable vector paths rather than pixels. Core tools include the Pen tool for precision path drawing, advanced typography controls, gradient and pattern tools, and an extensive library of brushes and effects. Illustrator integrates with the rest of Creative Cloud, sharing assets with Photoshop, InDesign, and After Effects through Creative Cloud Libraries, and includes AI-assisted features such as Generative Recolor, which uses Adobe Firefly to recolor artwork from text prompts. An iPad version of Illustrator lets users draw and edit vector artwork on a tablet with Apple Pencil support. Illustrator is sold as a subscription: a single-app plan starts around $22.99/month on an annual commitment billed monthly (or roughly $34.49/month with no annual commitment), while the Creative Cloud All Apps plan bundling Illustrator with 20+ other apps runs about $59.99/month annually. Team and student/teacher pricing is also available. Illustrator is aimed at graphic designers, brand and logo designers, illustrators, and print/packaging designers who need precision vector tools, and it remains a standard tool in professional design workflows alongside Photoshop.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adobe Illustrator is a vector graphics editor built around paths rather than pixels, letting artwork scale to any size without quality loss.
First released in 1987, it remains one of Adobe's flagship design applications and the industry standard for logo and icon design.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Illustrator's Pen tool, typography controls, and gradient/pattern tools give designers precise control over vector artwork, and it shares assets with other Creative Cloud apps through Creative Cloud Libraries.
Generative Recolor, powered by Adobe Firefly, lets users recolor artwork from text prompts, and an iPad app supports drawing and editing with Apple Pencil.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: A single-app plan starts around $22.99/month on an annual commitment billed monthly (about $34.49/month without an annual commitment). The Creative Cloud All Apps plan runs about $59.99/month annually, and a Team plan is available around $37.99/user/month.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Illustrator is aimed at graphic designers, brand and logo designers, illustrators, and print or packaging designers who need scalable, precision vector artwork.

features.title: Vector Drawing
features.description: Precision Pen tool and shape tools for scalable artwork.

features.title: Typography Tools
features.description: Advanced type and font controls for branding and print.

features.title: Generative Recolor
features.description: AI-assisted recoloring of artwork via Adobe Firefly.

features.title: Creative Cloud Libraries
features.description: Share colors, assets, and brushes across Adobe apps.

features.title: iPad App
features.description: Draw and edit vector art on tablet with Apple Pencil support.

features.title: Effects & Brushes Library
features.description: Extensive built-in effects, brushes, and pattern tools.

pros: Industry-standard vector tool with deep precision control
Strong integration with the rest of Creative Cloud
iPad app extends the workflow to tablets
Regular feature updates including AI tools
cons: Subscription-only, no perpetual license option
Steep learning curve for beginners
Can be expensive as a single app versus bundled suites for casual users

faqs.question: Can I buy Illustrator outright instead of subscribing?
faqs.answer: No, Adobe only offers Illustrator via subscription; there's no one-time purchase option.

faqs.question: Does Illustrator work on iPad?
faqs.answer: Yes, Adobe offers a dedicated Illustrator app for iPad.

faqs.question: What's the difference between Illustrator and Photoshop?
faqs.answer: Illustrator creates scalable vector graphics built from paths, while Photoshop edits pixel-based raster images like photos.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Adobe offers a free trial period for Illustrator before requiring a paid subscription.

use_cases.title: Logo and brand identity design
use_cases.description: Designers create scalable logos and brand assets that work at any size.

use_cases.title: Illustration and digital art
use_cases.description: Illustrators produce vector artwork, icons, and character designs.

use_cases.title: Print and packaging design
use_cases.description: Designers prepare vector-based layouts for print and packaging production.

enrichment.short_description: Adobe Photoshop is the industry-standard raster image editing app for photo retouching, compositing, and digital art, offered via Creative Cloud subscription.
enrichment.seo_title: Adobe Photoshop: Photo & Image Editing Software
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Adobe Photoshop is the leading pixel-based image editor for retouching, compositing, and design. Plans start around $9.99/month with Lightroom.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Adobe Photoshop is a raster image editing application made by Adobe Inc., originally released in 1990, widely used for photo retouching, compositing, digital painting, and graphic design. It works with pixel-based images organized in layers, and includes tools for selection, masking, color correction, and filters. Photoshop's Generative Fill and Generative Expand features, powered by Adobe Firefly, let users add, remove, or extend image content using text prompts, and Neural Filters apply AI-driven effects like skin smoothing or sky replacement. Photoshop integrates with Adobe Lightroom for photo management and with Creative Cloud Libraries for sharing assets across apps, and an iPad version supports touch and Apple Pencil editing. Photoshop is subscription-only: the standalone app costs about $22.99/month on an annual plan billed monthly, while the Photography Plan bundling Photoshop with Lightroom starts at $9.99/month (20GB cloud storage) or $19.99/month (1TB storage). It's also included in the Creative Cloud All Apps plan. Photoshop is used by photographers, graphic designers, digital artists, and marketing teams that need precise photo editing and compositing, and it remains the dominant tool in professional image editing, with 'photoshop' having become a generic verb for photo manipulation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Adobe Photoshop is a pixel-based (raster) image editor, first released in 1990, that remains the standard tool for photo retouching, compositing, and digital art.
Images are organized in editable layers, with dedicated tools for selection, masking, color correction, and filters.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Generative Fill and Generative Expand, powered by Adobe Firefly, let users add, remove, or extend image content from text prompts, while Neural Filters apply AI-driven effects like skin smoothing or sky replacement.
Photoshop integrates with Lightroom for photo management and Creative Cloud Libraries for shared assets, and an iPad app supports touch and Apple Pencil editing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: The Photography Plan bundling Photoshop with Lightroom starts at $9.99/month (20GB storage) or $19.99/month (1TB storage). The standalone Photoshop app costs about $22.99/month, and it's also included in the Creative Cloud All Apps plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Photoshop is used by photographers, digital artists, graphic designers, and marketing teams that need precise photo editing, retouching, and compositing.

features.title: Layer-Based Editing
features.description: Non-destructive editing using layers, masks, and adjustment layers.

features.title: Generative Fill & Expand
features.description: AI-powered content generation and extension from text prompts.

features.title: Neural Filters
features.description: AI-powered effects such as skin smoothing and sky replacement.

features.title: Selection & Masking Tools
features.description: Precise object selection and masking for compositing.

features.title: Lightroom Integration
features.description: Sync photos and edits with Adobe Lightroom.

features.title: iPad App
features.description: Touch and Apple Pencil editing on iPad.

pros: Most widely supported image-editing format and plugin ecosystem
Powerful AI generative tools for compositing
Deep integration with Lightroom and Creative Cloud
Available on both desktop and iPad
cons: Subscription-only pricing, no perpetual license
Resource-intensive; requires a capable computer
Steep learning curve for the full feature set

faqs.question: Can I buy Photoshop as a one-time purchase?
faqs.answer: No, Photoshop is subscription-only through Adobe Creative Cloud.

faqs.question: What is Generative Fill?
faqs.answer: An AI feature that lets users add, remove, or extend image content using text prompts, powered by Adobe Firefly.

faqs.question: Does Photoshop include Lightroom?
faqs.answer: The Photography Plan bundles Photoshop with Lightroom starting at $9.99/month.

faqs.question: Is there an iPad version?
faqs.answer: Yes, Adobe offers a Photoshop app for iPad.

use_cases.title: Photo retouching and editing
use_cases.description: Photographers retouch and color-correct images for clients.

use_cases.title: Digital compositing and art
use_cases.description: Artists combine and manipulate images to create composite artwork.

use_cases.title: Marketing and social graphics
use_cases.description: Teams edit product photos and create graphics for campaigns.

features: Full Photoshop desktop and iPad app

enrichment.short_description: ADP Workforce Now is a cloud HR platform combining payroll, benefits administration, time tracking, and talent management for small and midsized businesses.
enrichment.seo_title: ADP Workforce Now: HR & Payroll Platform
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ADP Workforce Now unifies payroll, HR, benefits, and time tracking in one cloud platform for small and midsized businesses. Custom pricing.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ADP Workforce Now is a cloud-based human capital management (HCM) platform from ADP (Automatic Data Processing), a payroll and HR services company founded in 1949 and headquartered in Roseland, New Jersey. Workforce Now unifies payroll processing, HR management, benefits administration, time and attendance tracking, and talent management (recruiting, onboarding, and performance) in a single platform aimed at small and midsized businesses, roughly 1 to 999 employees, though ADP also serves larger enterprises through related products. The platform includes AI-assisted features such as payroll anomaly detection, turnover prediction, and compensation benchmarking drawing on ADP's payroll database covering millions of U.S. employees. It's sold in tiered packages -- Select, Plus, and Premium -- with each tier adding more functionality, such as full time-and-attendance tracking in the Premium tier; pricing is not published and requires a custom quote from ADP. Workforce Now is accessible via a web dashboard and ADP's mobile apps, and integrates with third-party business applications through the ADP Marketplace. It's used by HR and payroll teams that want to consolidate multiple HR functions into one system rather than running separate payroll, benefits, and HR software.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ADP Workforce Now is a cloud human capital management platform from ADP, a payroll processing company founded in 1949, aimed at consolidating payroll, HR, benefits, and time tracking into one system.
It's built primarily for businesses with 1 to 999 employees, with ADP offering separate products for larger enterprises.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core modules cover payroll processing with automated tax filing, HR record-keeping and onboarding, time and attendance tracking, benefits administration, and talent management for recruiting and performance.
AI-assisted features include payroll anomaly detection, turnover prediction, and compensation benchmarking drawn from ADP's payroll dataset.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: Workforce Now is sold in three tiers -- Select, Plus, and Premium -- with Premium adding full time and attendance tracking. ADP does not publish pricing; cost depends on company size and modules selected, and requires a custom quote.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Workforce Now is aimed at HR and payroll teams at small and midsized businesses that want to replace multiple point solutions with one integrated HCM platform.

features.title: Payroll Processing
features.description: Automated payroll calculation, tax filing, and AI anomaly detection.

features.title: HR Management
features.description: Centralized employee records, onboarding, and HR workflows.

features.title: Time & Attendance
features.description: Time tracking and scheduling, included in the Premium tier.

features.title: Benefits Administration
features.description: Manage benefits enrollment and carrier connections.

features.title: Talent Management
features.description: Recruiting, onboarding, and performance management tools.

features.title: Compensation Benchmarking
features.description: Salary comparison data drawn from ADP's payroll dataset.

features.title: ADP Marketplace Integrations
features.description: Connects with third-party business applications.

features.title: Mobile Access
features.description: ADP mobile apps for employees and managers.

pros: Consolidates payroll, HR, benefits, and time tracking in one platform
Backed by ADP's decades of payroll compliance experience
Compensation benchmarking data is a differentiator versus smaller HR tools
Scales via tiered plans as a business grows
cons: Pricing isn't published; requires a sales quote
Tiered structure means some features, like full time tracking, require upgrading to Premium
Implementation and per-tier feature gating can add complexity versus simpler payroll tools

faqs.question: How much does ADP Workforce Now cost?
faqs.answer: ADP doesn't publish pricing; cost depends on company size, modules selected, and requires a custom quote.

faqs.question: What company sizes is Workforce Now for?
faqs.answer: It's built primarily for businesses with 1 to 999 employees, with ADP offering separate products for larger enterprises.

faqs.question: What are the plan tiers?
faqs.answer: Select, Plus, and Premium, with Premium adding full time and attendance tracking.

faqs.question: Does Workforce Now have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, employees and managers can access it through ADP's mobile apps.

use_cases.title: Unified payroll and HR for growing businesses
use_cases.description: Replaces separate payroll and HR systems with one integrated platform.
use_cases.audience: Small and midsized business HR/payroll teams

use_cases.title: Benefits and time tracking management
use_cases.description: Administers employee benefits enrollment and tracks time and attendance in one system.

use_cases.title: Talent acquisition and performance management
use_cases.description: Manages recruiting, onboarding, and performance reviews.

enrichment.short_description: Advanced Web Ranking (AWR) is an SEO rank-tracking platform that monitors keyword rankings across Google, Bing, YouTube, and AI search engines for agencies and SEO teams.
enrichment.seo_title: Advanced Web Ranking: SEO Rank Tracking Tool
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Advanced Web Ranking tracks keyword rankings, SERP features, and AI search visibility. Plans start at $139/month with a free trial.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Advanced Web Ranking (AWR) is an SEO rank-tracking and reporting platform made by Caphyon, a software company founded in 2002 and headquartered in Craiova, Romania. AWR tracks keyword rankings across Google, Bing, YouTube, Amazon, and regional search engines, with daily, weekly, or on-demand update schedules, and includes pixel-level SERP position tracking, click-through-rate estimates, and visibility metrics. It also offers AI search visibility tracking to monitor how sites appear in AI-generated search answers, SEO forecasting that compares organic traffic value to equivalent paid search costs, and competitor tracking for up to 50 rivals. AWR supports white-label, automated, and shareable reporting, and integrates with Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Bing Webmaster Tools, Looker Studio, and BigQuery, plus a developer API and a Chrome extension called AWR Search Anywhere. It's sold on tiered monthly plans -- Pro ($139/month, 7,000 keywords), Agency ($279/month, 14,500 keywords), Enterprise ($699/month, 35,500 keywords), and Enterprise Plus ($980/month, 50,000 keywords) -- with a 10% discount for annual billing and a 7-day free trial. AWR is aimed at SEO agencies, in-house SEO teams, and freelance SEO professionals who need detailed rank tracking and client-ready reporting at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Advanced Web Ranking (AWR) is an SEO rank-tracking platform built by Caphyon, a Romanian software company founded in 2002, with more than two decades of SERP-tracking experience.
It tracks keyword positions across multiple search engines and provides detailed SERP and visibility analytics beyond simple rank position.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: AWR tracks rankings across Google, Bing, YouTube, Amazon, and regional engines on daily, weekly, or on-demand schedules, with pixel-level SERP position tracking and CTR estimates.
It also offers AI search visibility tracking, SEO forecasting against paid search cost, tracking of up to 50 competitors, and white-label, automated, and shareable reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: AWR is sold on four monthly tiers: Pro at $139/month (7,000 keywords), Agency at $279/month (14,500 keywords), Enterprise at $699/month (35,500 keywords), and Enterprise Plus at $980/month (50,000 keywords). Annual billing saves 10%, and a 7-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: AWR is aimed at SEO agencies, in-house SEO teams, and freelance SEO professionals who need detailed rank tracking, competitor monitoring, and client-ready reporting.

features.title: Multi-Engine Rank Tracking
features.description: Tracks rankings on Google, Bing, YouTube, Amazon, and regional search engines.

features.title: Pixel-Level SERP Tracking
features.description: Tracks search result position to the pixel with CTR estimates.

features.title: AI Search Visibility Tracking
features.description: Monitors how sites appear in AI-generated search answers.

features.title: SEO Forecasting
features.description: Compares organic traffic value against equivalent Google Ads cost.

features.title: Competitor Tracking
features.description: Tracks up to 50 competitors at no extra cost.

features.title: White-Label Reporting
features.description: Automated, shareable, and white-label client reports.

features.title: Integrations & API
features.description: Connects with Google Analytics, Search Console, Looker Studio, BigQuery, and a developer API.

features.title: Chrome Extension
features.description: AWR Search Anywhere extension for quick rank checks.

pros: Granular, pixel-level SERP tracking beyond basic rank position
Strong white-label reporting for agencies
Broad integration ecosystem, including Looker Studio and BigQuery
Unlimited projects and users on paid plans
cons: No permanent free tier, only a 7-day free trial
Pricing scales quickly with keyword volume needs
Primarily positioned toward agencies, which may be more than solo or small-site users need

faqs.question: Does Advanced Web Ranking have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, it offers a 7-day free trial with no credit card required, but no permanent free tier.

faqs.question: How much does AWR cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $139/month for the Pro tier (7,000 keywords), up to $980/month for Enterprise Plus (50,000 keywords).

faqs.question: Does AWR track AI search results?
faqs.answer: Yes, it includes AI search visibility tracking alongside traditional SERP tracking.

faqs.question: Can I track competitors?
faqs.answer: Yes, up to 50 competitors are included at no extra cost.

use_cases.title: Agency client reporting
use_cases.description: Agencies track rankings for multiple clients and generate white-label reports.

use_cases.title: Enterprise SEO tracking at scale
use_cases.description: Large sites track tens of thousands of keywords with integrations like BigQuery.

use_cases.title: Competitive SEO analysis
use_cases.description: Teams monitor competitor rankings and SERP feature ownership.

enrichment.short_description: AdvancedMD is a cloud-based platform combining electronic health records, practice management, and medical billing for independent healthcare practices.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: AdvancedMD combines EHR, scheduling, billing, and telehealth for independent medical practices. Pricing available by quote.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: AdvancedMD is a cloud-based healthcare practice management and electronic health records (EHR) platform made by AdvancedMD, Inc., a company founded in 1999 and headquartered in South Jordan, Utah. The platform combines clinical documentation, practice management, medical billing, and patient engagement tools for independent medical practices and specialty clinics. Clinical features include AI-enabled and ambient documentation, electronic prescribing, and specialty-specific charting templates. Practice management tools cover appointment scheduling, real-time insurance eligibility verification, and claims management, while revenue cycle management (RCM) services handle claim scrubbing and accounts-receivable tracking, offered either as software or as a managed billing service. Patient-facing features include a patient portal, telehealth visits, automated appointment reminders, and secure messaging. AdvancedMD is hosted on AWS, is HIPAA-compliant, and holds ONC Health IT certification, and it supports MACRA/MIPS quality reporting. The platform is used by independent practices across specialties such as mental health, physical therapy, and orthopedics, as well as medical billing companies and larger multi-location group practices, and includes mobile apps for clinical and practice-management functions. AdvancedMD does not publish standard pricing; prospective customers must request a custom quote, and the company has offered promotions such as free introductory months for certain plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: AdvancedMD is a cloud-based practice management and EHR platform from AdvancedMD, Inc., founded in 1999 and headquartered in South Jordan, Utah.
It combines clinical documentation, practice management, billing, and patient engagement in one connected system for independent healthcare practices.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Clinical tools include AI-enabled and ambient documentation, e-prescribing, and specialty-specific templates, while practice management covers scheduling, real-time eligibility verification, and claims management.

Revenue cycle management services handle claim scrubbing and A/R tracking, available as software or a managed billing service, and patient-facing tools include a portal, telehealth, and secure messaging.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Overview
paragraphs: AdvancedMD does not publish standard pricing on its website; the company directs prospective customers to request a custom quote, with cost depending on practice size, specialty, and modules selected.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: AdvancedMD serves independent and specialty medical practices, including mental health, physical therapy, and orthopedics providers, as well as medical billing companies and multi-location group practices.

features.title: EHR & Clinical Documentation
features.description: AI-enabled and ambient notes, e-prescribing, and specialty-specific charting templates.

features.title: Practice Management
features.description: Scheduling, real-time insurance eligibility verification, and claims management.

features.title: Revenue Cycle Management
features.description: Claim scrubbing, accounts-receivable tracking, and managed billing services.

features.title: Patient Engagement
features.description: Patient portal, telehealth visits, appointment reminders, and secure messaging.

features.title: Analytics & Reporting
features.description: Real-time financial and clinical dashboards across multiple locations.

features.title: Mobile Apps
features.description: Clinical and practice-management access on the go.

features.title: Compliance
features.description: HIPAA-compliant, ONC-certified, and MACRA/MIPS ready.

pros: Combines EHR, practice management, and billing in one connected platform
Specialty-specific templates for various practice types
Offers both self-service software and managed billing service options
ONC-certified and built for regulatory compliance (MACRA/MIPS)
cons: No public pricing; requires a sales quote to get costs
Third-party estimates suggest per-provider costs can be significant for full EHR plus practice management bundles
As an all-in-one platform, it may be more than very small practices need

faqs.question: Does AdvancedMD publish its pricing?
faqs.answer: No, pricing isn't publicly listed; practices need to request a quote based on modules and provider count.

faqs.question: Is AdvancedMD only for large practices?
faqs.answer: No, it's designed primarily for independent and specialty practices of varying sizes, as well as billing companies.

faqs.question: Does AdvancedMD offer telehealth?
faqs.answer: Yes, telehealth visits are included among its patient engagement tools.

faqs.question: Is AdvancedMD HIPAA-compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, and its EHR holds ONC Health IT certification.

use_cases.title: Independent practice EHR and billing
use_cases.description: Solo and small-group practices manage charting, scheduling, and claims in one system.

use_cases.title: Specialty practice workflows
use_cases.description: Mental health, physical therapy, and other specialty practices use tailored templates and workflows.

use_cases.title: Outsourced medical billing
use_cases.description: Billing companies use AdvancedMD's RCM tools to manage claims for multiple provider clients.
